title: Add unregister_user to the registration module
description:
Repository: https://github.com/MuhammedSenn/demo-user-service-python
Branch: main

Requirement:
The registration module can register and look up users but cannot remove one. Add an unregister_user(email) function in registration.py that normalizes the email the same way register_user does (trim surrounding whitespace and lowercase) and removes it from the users list if present. Return True if a user was removed, or False if the email was not registered. Do not change the existing register_user, get_user, or get_user_count behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:
- unregister_user removes a registered email (matched case-insensitively) and returns True
- unregister_user returns False for an email that is not registered
- After unregistering, get_user returns None and get_user_count decreases by one
- Existing registration, lookup, and count behavior is unchanged
- Add or update unit tests in test_registration.py covering case-insensitive removal and the not-found case
